name: Monitor Workflow and Signal a Task
query: Check the status of my running workflows and complete the approval task in workflow abc-123
expected_behavior: Step 1: Check if `conductor` CLI is available
Step 2: Search for running workflows using `conductor workflow search -s RUNNING -c 10`
Step 3: Present a summary of running workflows with workflowId, name, and startTime
Step 4: Get detailed execution of workflow abc-123 using `conductor workflow get-execution abc-123 -c`
Step 5: Identify the pending WAIT or HUMAN task (the approval task) from the task list
Step 6: Signal the task using `conductor task signal --workflow-id abc-123 --task-ref <taskRefName> --status COMPLETED --output '{"approved": true}'`
Step 7: Verify the workflow progressed by checking status again
Step 8: Report the updated workflow status to the user
success_criteria: Running workflows are searched and listed before acting on a specific one
Workflow execution is fetched with task details to identify the pending task
Plan describes fetching execution details first and extracting the task reference name from them, rather than assuming a task name
Signal command uses the correct workflowId, taskRefName, and status
Output JSON is provided with the signal to advance the workflow
Workflow status is verified after signaling to confirm it progressed
Summary includes before/after status of the workflow
